Title: Sufficient coumarin accumulation improves apple resistance to Cytospora mali under high-potassium status

Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: antifungal defense; improves resistance to Phytophthora parasitica
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: promotes coumarin-associated resistance to P. parasitica under high-K; silencing reduces resistance and overexpression increases resistance
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. NbBGLU40 was functionally tested in N. benthamiana as a coumarin-synthesis-related gene under different K conditions. 2. VIGS silencing of NbBGLU40 in high-K plants enlarged P. parasitica lesions and caused resistance loss. 3. Transient overexpression of NbBGLU40 in low-K plants reduced lesion size, supporting a positive role in coumarin-mediated defense.
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: VIGS silencing; transient overexpression; RT-qPCR; pathogen lesion assay; metabolomics/contextual coumarin evidence
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: tested in leaves under high-K and low-K conditions
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: high potassium vs low potassium; Phytophthora parasitica infection
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: The paper frames NbBGLU40 as encoding beta-glucosidase 40 for coumarin synthesis in tobacco, but direct enzymatic assay was not reported in this study; used mainly as conservation evidence in a second species.
Common_Genes.Omics_Data: untargeted metabolomics for N. benthamiana; RNA-seq accession CRA009344 pertains apple, not NbBGLU40 experiment
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Candidate engineering target to improve coumarin-linked disease resistance under suboptimal K conditions in Solanaceae or as proof of concept for cross-species deployment.

Pathway_Genes.Applied_Species_Variety: 'Orin' calli; 'Fuji' trees on M.9 rootstock for branch studies
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: antifungal defense; inhibits Cytospora mali growth and strengthens resistance to Cytospora canker
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: mainly expressed in branches and calli, followed by leaves and fruit; strongly induced under high-K; rapidly induced after pathogen challenge
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: mainly expressed in branches and calli, followed by leaves and fruit; strongly induced under high-K; rapidly induced after pathogen challenge
Pathway_Genes.Metabolic_Step_Position: hub step; downstream/terminal release step in coumarin formation
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: supported as key control/hub step for coumarin accumulation in this study
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: promotes coumarin accumulation and increases resistance to C. mali; RNAi reduces coumarin and resistance
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. MdBGLU40 was the most strongly induced coumarin-synthesis candidate in high-K apple tissue, showing about 13.1-fold higher expression than in low-K tissue. 2. Overexpression of MdBGLU40 in apple calli elevated coumarin to near high-K wild-type levels and restored strong resistance even under low-K conditions. 3. RNAi suppression of MdBGLU40 in high-K calli reduced coumarin content and increased lesion size, demonstrating in vivo requirement for high resistance. 4. MdBGLU40 is directly activated by MdMYB1r1 under high-K status.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: RT-qPCR; transgenic overexpression; RNAi silencing; western blotting; coumarin quantification by LC-MS; pathogen lesion assay; coexpression analysis
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: high potassium vs low potassium; TEA potassium channel inhibitor; Cytospora mali infection
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: 35S constitutive promoter for overexpression; RNAi construct for suppression
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: High-value engineering target for improving apple resistance to Cytospora canker by boosting coumarin accumulation; suitable for transgenic overexpression or activation editing, potentially combined with optimized K fertilization and upstream regulator MdMYB1r1.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: The paper explicitly cites beta-glucosyl-2-coumarinate-to-coumarin conversion by BGLU as the hub step in coumarin synthesis; no direct purified-enzyme assay for MdBGLU40 itself was presented.
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq (NGDC CRA009344); proteomics; targeted metabolomics; WGCNA

Pathway_Genes.Applied_Species_Variety: 'Fuji' trees on M.9 rootstock; 'Orin' calli context
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: antifungal defense via coumarin pathway context
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: higher in high-K tissues than low-K; mainly expressed in calli and branches
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: higher in high-K tissues than low-K; mainly expressed in calli and branches
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: putative hydrolysis step contributing to coumarin release
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: positively associated with high-K coumarin biosynthesis, but not functionally validated as decisive
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. MdBGLU12 was identified as one of the apple coumarin-synthesis-related genes. 2. Its transcript level was higher in high-K than low-K tissue. 3. It showed tissue-specific expression, mainly in calli and branches.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Secondary candidate for pathway stacking with MdBGLU40, but causal value remains weaker due to lack of direct functional tests.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Included as a coumarin-pathway candidate, but the central mechanistic experiments focused on MdBGLU40 rather than MdBGLU12.

Pathway_Genes.Applied_Species_Variety: 'Fuji' trees on M.9 rootstock; 'Orin' calli context
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: roots primarily; coumarin pathway context in apple tissues
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: antifungal defense via coumarin pathway context
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: higher in high-K than low-K tissues; highly expressed in roots
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: higher in high-K than low-K tissues; highly expressed in roots
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: putative beta-glucosidase step contributing to coumarin release
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: positively associated with high-K phenylpropanoid/coumarin pathway activation
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. MdBGLU42 was identified among candidate coumarin-synthesis genes in apple. 2. It was induced by high-K status and by pathogen challenge in expression analyses. 3. Its expression was tissue-specific, with strongest expression in roots.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: RT-qPCR expression profiling; annotation-based candidate identification
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: high potassium vs low potassium; TEA treatment; pathogen challenge
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Possible supportive target for tissue-specific coumarin engineering, particularly root-biased contexts; causal resistance value in branches not directly proven here.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: The paper references Arabidopsis BGLU42 as important for root coumarin synthesis, supporting homologous inference, but no direct transgenic test of MdBGLU42 was performed.

Pathway_Genes.Applied_Species_Variety: 'Fuji' trees on M.9 rootstock; 'Orin' calli context
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: antifungal defense via coumarin pathway context
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: higher in high-K tissues than low-K; mainly expressed in calli and branches
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: higher in high-K tissues than low-K; mainly expressed in calli and branches
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: putative hydrolysis step contributing to coumarin release
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: positively associated with coumarin biosynthesis under high-K conditions
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. MdBGLU47 was among the coumarin-pathway genes elevated in high-K apple tissue. 2. It displayed tissue-specific expression, mainly in calli and branches. 3. It was monitored as part of the high-K-induced coumarin biosynthetic module, but not directly functionally validated.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: RT-qPCR expression profiling; annotation-based pathway assignment
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: high potassium vs low potassium; TEA treatment
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Auxiliary candidate for pathway stacking, pending direct functional confirmation.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Not a principal mechanistic gene compared with MdBGLU40.

Pathway_Genes.Applied_Species_Variety: 'Fuji' trees on M.9 rootstock; 'Orin' calli context
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: antifungal defense via coumarin pathway context
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: higher in high-K tissues than low-K; highly expressed in roots
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: higher in high-K tissues than low-K; highly expressed in roots
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: putatively glycosylates coumarin precursors in the biosynthetic route leading to coumarin storage/release intermediates
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: associated with high-K coumarin biosynthesis but not directly validated as determinant of resistance
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. MdUGT88a1 was identified as one of two apple UGT genes potentially involved in coumarin synthesis. 2. Its transcript was higher in high-K tissue than low-K tissue. 3. Expression was root-biased, indicating tissue-specific coumarin pathway organization.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential upstream partner for multi-gene coumarin engineering, though direct causal evidence in branch resistance is limited.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: The study did not directly manipulate MdUGT88a1; its role is inferred from annotation and expression.

Regulation_Genes.Applied_Species_Variety: 'Orin' calli; 'Fuji' trees on M.9 rootstock for branch studies
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: antifungal defense; suppresses Cytospora mali growth by enabling sufficient coumarin accumulation
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: activates MdBGLU40 expression by binding the MdBGLU40 promoter, especially the P1 site containing CAACGG; also increases promoter activity more strongly under high-K
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: major positive regulator and hub TF controlling coumarin synthesis under high-K status
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: promotes coumarin accumulation and enhances resistance to C. mali; silencing reduces coumarin and resistance
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. WGCNA identified MdMYB1r1 as the hub transcription factor most strongly correlated with coumarin synthesis traits and MdBGLU40 expression. 2. MdMYB1r1 is nuclear localized and highly induced in high-K apple tissue. 3. Transient and stable overexpression increased MdBGLU40 expression, coumarin accumulation, and resistance even under low-K conditions. 4. VIGS/RNAi silencing reduced MdBGLU40 expression, coumarin content, and resistance, including under high-K conditions. 5. Luciferase, Y1H, ChIP-qPCR, EMSA, and ITC demonstrated direct binding to the MdBGLU40 promoter, with stronger binding/activity under high-K conditions.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: WGCNA coexpression analysis; transient overexpression; VIGS silencing; stable overexpression and RNAi; subcellular localization; luciferase reporter assay; Y1H; ChIP-qPCR; EMSA; ITC; RT-qPCR; metabolite profiling; pathogen lesion assay
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: high potassium vs low potassium; Cytospora mali infection
Regulation_Genes.Applied_Promoters: 35S constitutive promoter for overexpression; RNAi and VIGS constructs for suppression
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: high in branches, calli, and fruit; about 13-fold higher in high-K than low-K apple tissue
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: high in branches, calli, and fruit; about 13-fold higher in high-K than low-K apple tissue
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Excellent regulatory target for engineering coumarin-based disease resistance in apple; activation or overexpression could be combined with MdBGLU40 and K management for stronger, environmentally supported resistance.
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Binding enrichment and promoter activation are stronger under high-K, indicating nutrient-status-dependent regulatory efficacy rather than simple constitutive regulation.
